purpose: Source of truth for Kaze exports, visual detection rules, component mapping, fake-name blocking, and on-prem AI screen pack generation.

visualHints: round/circular image in a header or user row
componentDetectionRules.useWhen: Use Avatar for user/profile/account identity display, circular profile images, user photos, account icons, or initials.

visualHints: visible label/action that can be clicked
componentDetectionRules.useWhen: Use Button for clickable actions, including primary actions, secondary actions, icon actions, quick actions, submit actions, reset actions, and export actions.

visualHints: short field with one line of typed text
componentDetectionRules.useWhen: Use TextField for single-line text inputs, search bars, prompt bars, chat inputs, filters, and short form fields.

visualHints: taller input with space for multiple lines
componentDetectionRules.useWhen: Use TextArea for large or multi-line text input areas, notes fields, descriptions, comments, and long prompt boxes.
Use Dropdown for single option selection, select fields, filters, and dropdown-style fields.

visualHints: selector that shows checked items or multiple selected values
componentDetectionRules.useWhen: Use CheckboxDropdown for dropdown controls that allow multiple checkbox selections, column selection, or multi-select filtering.
Use Checkbox for checkbox-style boolean inputs.
Use Toggle for switch-style boolean settings.
Use Radio for a single radio option.
Use RadioGroup for grouped radio options where only one option can be selected.
Use Segmented for compact selection between related modes or views.
Use Slider for selecting a value from a range.
Use Typography for headings, titles, labels, paragraphs, helper text, descriptions, and captions.
Use Tag for metadata labels, categories, and status pills.
Use Badge for small counts, notification markers, or compact status indicators.
Use Lozenge for compact status/state labels.
Use Pills for pill-style option groups or compact filter selections.
Use Swatch for colour samples, theme colour selection, or visual colour indicators.
Use Table for simple tabular layouts and basic data tables.

visualHints: enterprise grid with sorting/filtering/large row set
componentDetectionRules.useWhen: Use AgGridTable for complex enterprise tables, sortable grids, filterable grids, or large admin data tables.
Use Modal for dialogs, popups, and confirmation overlays.
Use Alert for inline warning, error, success, and info messages.
Use Toast for temporary feedback messages.
Use Notification for notification display patterns.
Use Tooltip for hover hints and short contextual help.
Use Upload for file upload and drag-and-drop upload areas.
Use Tabs for switching between related sections.
Use Breadcrumb for hierarchical page navigation.
Use Pagination for paginated lists and tables.

visualHints: three dots or menu trigger that opens action list
componentDetectionRules.useWhen: Use ContextMenu for contextual action menus.
Use Collapse for expandable/collapsible content sections.
Use Progress for progress bars and completion indicators.
Use Steps for multi-step flows, wizards, and process indicators.
Use Datepicker for date selection and date input fields.
Use Timepicker for time selection and time input fields.

ambiguityRules.when: The input appears single-line, compact, search-like, or prompt-bar-like.
The input is visibly large, multi-line, notes-like, comment-like, or description-like.
The table is simple, static, or has only basic rows and columns.
The grid is complex, enterprise-style, sortable, filterable, or has many columns.
The visual element is a category, status label, metadata chip, or pill label.
The visual element is a number, count bubble, notification marker, or compact count indicator.

unconfirmedPatterns.recommendedFallback: Use existing project layout/navigation pattern. Do not invent a Kaze Sidebar component.
Use project layout wrappers or semantic HTML layout. Do not invent KazeBox, KazeFlex, or KazeLayout.
Use TextField for single-line prompt input or TextArea for large multi-line prompt input.
Use existing project icon pattern. Do not invent KazeIcon.
Use existing project card/panel pattern or semantic layout wrappers. Do not invent KazeCard unless package exports prove it exists.

generatorRules: Use this catalog as the source of truth for Kaze exports.
Do not let the model invent Kaze component names.
Ask the vision model to detect generic UI roles first.
Map detected visual roles to Kaze exports using componentDetectionRules and patternMappings.
If a detected visual role matches a mandatoryMappingRules entry, the mapped Kaze component must appear in handoff.md and kaze-component-mapping.md.
If a component is not in confirmedExports, do not present it as a valid Kaze export.
If an unknown visual pattern appears, document it as a fallback instead of inventing a component.

validatorRules.rule: TextField or TextArea required for text input
CheckboxDropdown required for multi-select dropdown controls
Table or AgGridTable required for tables/grids
Alert required for inline status messages

detectedElements.recommendedKazeExport: one of confirmedExports or Unknown / verify from Kaze

onPremPromptRules: Use exact Kaze export names from confirmedExports only.
Detect visible UI roles first, then map roles through patternMappings.
If a visual role appears in mandatoryMappingRules, the mapped export is mandatory.
Return compact tables or JSON. Do not invent APIs, routes, auth, database logic, permissions, or fake components.
Do not describe avatar/button/input/text only generically; include Avatar/Button/TextField/TextArea/Typography when applicable.
automaticFailConditions: Uses fake Kaze-prefixed exports as valid imports.
Says Button, TextField, Dropdown, Avatar, or Typography are fake invalid names.
Detects profile/avatar visual element but does not mention Avatar.
Detects clickable actions but does not mention Button.
Detects text input but does not mention TextField or TextArea.
Detects dropdown/select controls but does not mention Dropdown.
Invents a Kaze component not listed in confirmedExports.
Invents route paths, backend APIs, authentication logic, permissions, or database logic.
notes: Swatch is exported as ColourSwatch as Swatch internally.
TextArea is exported as TextAreaField as TextArea internally.
notification and useNotification are utility/hook exports, not visual components.
